message:
I've never been the 'prepared' friend. I'm the one who forgets her reusable bags, shows up without an umbrella, you know the type.

But last month I actually *talked* my family through where we'd go if there was a fire. We walked around, identified which window we'd use in each room, made sure the kids knew to meet outside by the mailbox. My husband looked at me like I'd grown a second head.

Then this week our neighbor's kitchen had a small grease fire. Everyone got out fine, but it hit different knowing *we* had a plan. For once, I wasn't scrambling or second-guessing—I just felt calm about it.

Turns out being prepared doesn't make you paranoid. It actually lets you relax a little. Wild.

Have you and your family talked through what you'd actually do? I was surprised how easy it was once we just sat down and did it.
